warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+ (ok=true, severity=warning): sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f- (ok=true, severity=warning): sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui-reactivity analysis on aniline (14 atoms) computed with DFT at B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard') via PySCF, gas phase, neutral reference charge 0 / multiplicity 1. The finite-difference Fukui method also computed the cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge -1, mult 2) as open-shell UKS states. Condensed Fukui functions were derived from Mulliken (PySCF) partial charges; geometry was the user-supplied aniline.xyz used as-is (single-point, no optimization). Headline result: the most electrophilic site is C3 (atom index 2, 0-based) with f+ = 2.2975867; the most nucleophilic site is N1 (atom index 0, 0-based) with f- = 0.20452359. Integrity status 'ok', trustworthy=true; charge-conservation checks passed with sum(f+) = 1.0000 and sum(f-) = 1.0000. The result JSON has no separate 'warnings' array; the three integrity checks (all ok=true, severity 'warning') carry the detail messages reproduced verbatim in the warnings field. Note: Mulliken-based condensed Fukui functions can produce noisy or out-of-range per-atom values (seen here), a known artifact of the Mulliken scheme; values are reported exactly as returned. Reproducibility: cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 .../aniline.xyz; result JSON aniline_fukui.json; plot at .../20260703-190618_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. No experimental comparison is offered.
